_meta.purpose: T3-0 read-only local ClientHello scoping: Go uTLS HelloChrome_Auto reference vs Rust hand-rolled Chrome emulation. Normalized / sanitized summary only.
_meta.method: Transparent local TCP recorder in front of the local REALITY fixture server (client->recorder->reality_server->tls_dest->http_target); 10 ClientHellos per kernel; temporary stdlib parser/normalizer (ephemeral, NOT committed). No public network. Raw bytes / ClientHello random / key_share key bytes / session_id value / token are excluded — only normalized structure, ordering, lengths, group types, GREASE positions, and JA3/JA4 digests.
_meta.reference: Go sing-box (-tags with_utls), fingerprint=chrome -> utls.HelloChrome_Auto (github.com/metacubex/utls); reality_client.go filters X25519MLKEM768.
_meta.candidate: Rust sb-tls/src/reality/handshake.rs build_client_config -> patched rustls ClientConfig.fingerprint=build_chrome_client_hello_fingerprint + session_id_generator=RealitySessionIdGenerator (REALITY auth in session_id).

levels.L1_tls_semantic_profile: MATCHED (every static profile field byte-identical, GREASE-stripped)
levels.L3_normalized_wire: NEAR-MATCH (2 residuals: GREASE cipher value; per-hello extension shuffle order)
levels.L4_raw_byte_identity: NOT A GOAL (random/key_share/session_id/GREASE-ECH/ext-order vary per-hello in BOTH)

documented_differences.camouflage_relevance: LOW — official JA3 ignores GREASE and the current local JA4-equivalent digest normalizes it; other passive/custom classifiers are out of scope. (JARM is a SERVER fingerprint, not a ClientHello-parity criterion.) Trivially fixable (randomize the GREASE cipher value).

documented_differences.go: shuffled per-hello (uTLS Chrome shuffle); 10 distinct orders
documented_differences.rust: shuffled per-hello (build_chrome_extension_order seed sampler); 10 distinct orders
documented_differences.classification: REALITY_PROTOCOL_VARIABILITY / EXPECTED_RANDOMNESS; shuffle DISTRIBUTION equivalence = UNKNOWN_REQUIRES_RESEARCH
documented_differences.camouflage_relevance: LOW at JA3/JA4 (both shuffle; no stable order to fingerprint). Distributional distinguishability is unproven and out of L2/L3 scope.

ja4_equivalent._note: STRUCTURAL APPROXIMATION (sorted, GREASE-stripped); not the official ja4 tool. Use as a regression digest only — JA4 cannot represent full extension payloads and is not by itself equivalent to real camouflage quality.

ja3_note: Classic AND grease-stripped JA3 are UNSTABLE for BOTH kernels (Chrome per-hello extension shuffle -> 10 distinct JA3 each; classic distinct=10/10). JA3 is therefore NOT a valid parity metric here — its known limitation. JA4 (sorted) is the right classifier-level metric and is identical across kernels.
must_stay_open: Extension-shuffle DISTRIBUTION equivalence (Rust seeded sampler vs uTLS PRNG) — UNKNOWN.
Real-network camouflage sufficiency (tier-2 observation) — a passing local JA4 parity does NOT prove a real censor cannot distinguish (active probing / traffic analysis).
HelloChrome_Auto auto-updates; the Rust profile is pinned to a Chrome snapshot (drift).
